01 — About

The background

I’m a freshman at NYU Stern in the Business, Technology & Entrepreneurship program, working toward a career in investment banking. My path here is unconventional: I built a comic book resale business generating $15K+ in revenue, funded by three years working as a McDonald’s Crew Trainer where I consistently outperformed 50+ competing locations. Those earnings opened my first brokerage account and kicked off a real obsession with how capital is deployed.

“I worked to fund a business. Used that business to fund my first trade. Then I got obsessed.”

At Stern I’m a JBiz Hedge Fund Fellow focused on merger arbitrage and financial modeling, and I’ve done product strategy consulting for startups. Previously I was president of Mock Trial — rebuilding the team through a full coach transition — and was selected as County Judge at American Legion Boys’ State, passing the bar exam as 1 of 18 out of 100+ applicants.

My edge is thinking like an operator. Understanding the friction of running a real business translates directly into how I analyze companies.

Investment Banking Financial Modeling Merger Arbitrage Private Credit Entrepreneurship Product Strategy
02 — Work

Selected projects

Private Credit · Real Estate PE2025–Present

Private Credit Cup — Team Apex Capital

Selected as a Real Estate PE & Credit Fellow at Project Destined — a Wall Street-backed real estate education program. Competed in the Private Credit Cup as Team Apex Capital, underwriting the Villages at Lake Silvercote deal. Built a full credit thesis using NOI analysis, cap rates, debt structuring, and equity waterfall modeling to assess investor returns. Presented a winning lending recommendation to industry professionals.

🏆 WinnerProject DestinedNOI AnalysisEquity Waterfall
Product Strategy · UX Research2025

Skiplist × BTE Design Sprint

Led research and go-to-market strategy for a BTE Design Sprint with Skiplist, a clothing resale platform. Conducted 20+ in-person interviews across Gen Z and Gen X demographics, built 6 customer personas with full journey maps, and produced a competitive teardown against eBay, Poshmark, ThredUp and The RealReal. Recommended three new product features and ranked five marketing channels by ROI.

20+ Interviews6 PersonasCompetitive Analysis
Entrepreneurship · Operations2020–Present

Pages & Panels

Founded and scaled a comic book resale business from zero to $15K+ in revenue and a 50+ dealer network. Managed all sourcing, pricing, inventory, and shipping independently — including international logistics and risk control on unsellable stock. Analyzed market trends to identify undervalued comics with strong resale demand. Entirely self-funded through McDonald’s earnings, which also opened my first brokerage account.

$15K+ Revenue50+ DealersSelf-Funded
